Reviews.Author: 15s30pfmfe
Reviews.Comment: Overall, I'd say SirHurt delivers a very strong performance across the board honestly. Its script support is one of its biggest strengths, executing almost all scripts with absolutely no issues whatsoever. And aside from the occasional lag, which is negligible, it remains highly consistent in this area. Reliability is another standout feature in my opinion. Its longevity (11 years!) and lean track record is another very strong feature of SirHurt, and makes it one of the most trustworthy options available at the moment. User experience is also very pleasant, as it has an interface that's easy to use, and more important, highly customizable with the ability to completely change the UI and even share custom designs with others and that makes it more flexible than any other executor I'm aware of. Updates are, in my opinion, its absolute strongest aspect though. SirHurt updates incredibly quickly, almost instantly in most cases, and consistently outperforms other executors when it comes to staying up-to-date and maintaining uptime. So, considering its very low price, cheaper than virtually any other paid executor currently available, it offers exceptional value in my opinion. When it comes to bang for your buck, SirHurt is the best option by far. I would highly recommend this executor.

Categories.Comment: When it comes to script support, my experience with SirHurt has been overwhelmingly positive. It handles the vast majority of scripts with ease to be honest, and there are hardly any that don't run. Of course, no executor is perfect. There are occasional moments of lag sometimes, and very rarely it may crash. However, these instances are infrequent and considering the price point, they're very easy to overlook. Another standout aspect is its extremely high UNC/SUNC support. Fun fact, SirHurt was the first executor to ever achieve 100% UNC, which really speaks to its reliability in executing scripts I'd say.

Regarding optimization, I'm aware there have been reports of instability, crashes, etc. which I can't personally speak for, as my own experience has been consistently smooth even since the beta V5. From my perspective, optimization has been excellent, with no major issues to note. That said, performance can definitely vary on the machine you're running it on, so others may have had different experiences. In my case though, it has been very stable overall. I do agree that there's still room for improvement when it comes to occasional lag. At times, it can require a bit of patience, but it's generally manageable and doesn't take away too much from the overall experience in my opinion.

SirHurt has been around since 2015, and I can confidently say it's not going anywhere anytime soon. Over its entire 11-year history, there have been zero credible reports of malware or scamming associated with it, which is a huge factor when considering trust obviously. And while its longevity isn't the only measure of reliability, it adds a very strong layer of confidence when it comes to concerns like bans or malware. In my opinion, SirHurt might just be one of the most trustworthy executors available. If there's one you can rely on in that regard, it's this one.

I think when it comes to user experience, SirHurt does a lot right. The interface is very user-friendly overall and it's very easy to navigate and use even if you're not experienced, and while the file management could use some work, it doesn't significantly get in the way of usability. One of the most commonly criticized aspects is the visual design though, which I'd say is often considered SirHurt's weakest point. Personally, though I actually really like it. My taste in executor UIs is admittedly a bit outdated so that likely plays a role but at the end of the day, visual design is highly subjective. So, in terms of pure ease of use, it's excellent. And importantly, the UI isn't something you're stuck with I should add, as SirHurt allows you to create and use your own custom interface, as well as use UIs made by others. So even if the default design isn't to your liking, there are plenty of alternatives available, so it's pretty difficult to judge in that area.

This is easily the area where SirHurt performs best in my opinion. It's consistently among the first (if not the very first) executors to update after changes. The margin isn't always huge, but the consistency is what stands out here anyway. Whenever a major update rolls out, SirHurt is almost always right there, ready to go, and quite often the updates are nearly instant! I think because of this, uptime is one of its strongest advantages and if your main concern is having an executor that stays up-to-date and usable with minimal downtime, then SirHurt is definitely the one to go for.
